description:
You are acting as a network engineer who has just inherited a jumble of
  configuration files under /home/user/netdiag.  
  Your objectives are focused on correct symbolic-link management and
  producing a strictly-formatted audit log.

  Starting filesystem snapshot (already present when you begin):
    1. Directory: /home/user/netdiag/configs
         ├── router.conf      – plain ASCII file
         └── switch.conf      – plain ASCII file
    2. Directory: /home/user/netdiag/logs             (empty)

  Goal – when you are completely finished the system must satisfy **all**
  of the following:

  A) Create the directory /home/user/netdiag/live_configs  
     • It must exist even if it was missing when you started.

  B) Inside /home/user/netdiag/live_configs create four symbolic links
     (exact spelling and relative/absolute targets matter!):

       1. router.conf   →  ../configs/router.conf
       2. switch.conf   →  ../configs/switch.conf
       3. dns.conf      →  /etc/resolv.conf
       4. firewall.conf →  ../configs/firewall.conf   (this intentionally
                          points at a non-existent target and must remain a
                          *broken* link to simulate a mis-configuration).

     Do NOT copy the files; they must be true symlinks.

  C) Produce an audit file named
       /home/user/netdiag/logs/symlink_audit.log

     Required format of symlink_audit.log (absolutely exact):
        • One entry per symlink, sorted alphabetically by the *link name*
          (dns.conf comes before firewall.conf, etc.).
        • Each entry on its own line with **exactly** this template:
              LINK_NAME:TARGET_PATH:STATUS
          where
              LINK_NAME  = name of the symbolic link (no path)
              TARGET_PATH = the *raw* string stored in the link
              STATUS      = “OK” if the target exists, otherwise “BROKEN”

        • After the four per-link lines, append a single summary line:
              TOTAL_LINKS:<count>, BROKEN:<count_broken>
          where the numbers are decimal integers.

     Example of correct final file (indentation added here only to show
     structure – the real file must have NO leading spaces):

       dns.conf:/etc/resolv.conf:OK
       firewall.conf:../configs/firewall.conf:BROKEN
       router.conf:../configs/router.conf:OK
       switch.conf:../configs/switch.conf:OK
       TOTAL_LINKS:4, BROKEN:1

     Additional log requirements:
       • Plain text, Unix line endings (LF).
       • File permissions must be 0644 or more restrictive.

  D) You may use any GNU/Linux user-space tools available in a typical
     Debian/Ubuntu environment.  You do NOT have root; work entirely under
     /home/user.

  Verification notes (for your awareness):
     • Automated tests will check that the four symlinks exist, point to
       exactly the targets defined in B), and that their type is “symbolic
       link”.
     • The test harness will read symlink_audit.log and validate every
       character, including order, spelling and counts.

  Make sure every requirement above is met before you consider the task
  complete.
truth:
INITIAL STATE (before the agent starts):

    Files and contents:
      /home/user/netdiag/configs/router.conf
         # Router config
         hostname R1
         interface Gi0/0
          ip address 192.168.1.1 255.255.255.0

      /home/user/netdiag/configs/switch.conf
         # Switch config
         hostname SW1
         vlan 10
          name USERS

    Directories:
      /home/user/netdiag/configs          – mode 0755
      /home/user/netdiag/logs             – mode 0755
      (No /home/user/netdiag/live_configs directory yet)

    No other files or links are present under /home/user/netdiag.

  EXPECTED FINAL STATE (after successful completion):

    1. Directory /home/user/netdiag/live_configs exists (mode ≥ 0755).

    2. Symlinks under live_configs (all in the same directory):
         router.conf   – symlink → ../configs/router.conf     (exists)
         switch.conf   – symlink → ../configs/switch.conf     (exists)
         dns.conf      – symlink → /etc/resolv.conf           (exists)
         firewall.conf – symlink → ../configs/firewall.conf   (broken)

    3. Audit file:
         Path: /home/user/netdiag/logs/symlink_audit.log
         Mode: 0644 or 0600
         Exact contents (5 lines total, newline-terminated):

           dns.conf:/etc/resolv.conf:OK
           firewall.conf:../configs/firewall.conf:BROKEN
           router.conf:../configs/router.conf:OK
           switch.conf:../configs/switch.conf:OK
           TOTAL_LINKS:4, BROKEN:1

    4. No extra files should be created anywhere inside /home/user/netdiag
       except those described above.
